findings.description: Tech-spec contains 20 Implementation Tasks (Wave 1 has 3, Wave 3 has 3, Wave 4 has 3, Wave 5 has 3, Audit Wave has 3, Final Wave has 3, plus Wave 2 with 2). The recommended ceiling is 15 tasks; above this threshold a tech-spec is usually a candidate for MVP / Extension splitting.
findings.recommendation: Surface this to the user for an explicit decision: (a) accept 20 tasks because each is genuinely atomic and required for the L-sized feature, or (b) split into MVP (contract + middleware core + facilitator + integration test + QA/deploy, ~13 tasks) and Extension (deploy script CLI niceties, README polish, npm publish, post-deploy expansion). User has already been informed; this is informational.

findings.description: Template specifies the subsection heading as `### What we're building/modifying`. Tech-spec uses `### What we're building`. Trivial deviation; both convey scope.
findings.recommendation: Rename to `### What we're building/modifying` for strict template compliance. No content change required.

findings.description: Description is a single long sentence enumerating every public function with its full signature, parameter list, modifiers, events, and inheritance chain. Functions are: `constructor`, `register`, `payWithAuthorization`, `withdraw`, `withdrawAll`, `getBalance`, `setFee`, `withdrawPlatformFees`, `pause`/`unpause`. This level of API-surface detail is closer to a task-file specification than to a tech-spec scope statement. Tech-spec rule: tasks describe scope; per-function detail belongs in the task file produced during decomposition.
findings.recommendation: Compress to 2-3 sentences naming the contract, its base classes, and the responsibility (e.g. `Implement PaymentSplitter â€” Solidity 0.8.20+ contract that wraps USDC.transferWithAuthorization, accounts per-developer balance with platform fee, supports owner-controlled fee/pause/withdrawal. Inherits OpenZeppelin Ownable, Pausable, ReentrancyGuard. Public surface listed in Data Models section.`). Move the full function list into the task file during task-decomposition.

findings.description: Description blends scope with sequencing (`missing header -> 402; decode -> verify -> settle -> 200-passthrough...`). This is borderline â€” pipeline order is part of the design (already in How it works) â€” but it edges towards implementation steps inside a tech-spec task.
findings.recommendation: Reduce to one-line scope (`Implement core.ts orchestrating the verify->settle pipeline, plus node-http and Fastify adapters, plus index.ts exports`). The detailed orchestration is already captured in the `## Architecture / How it works` sequence; do not duplicate.

findings.location: Task 13 (Deploy script + registration CLI + README) and Decision D11
findings.description: Task 13 hard-codes `initialFeeBps = 50` in the deploy call `PaymentSplitter(usdcAddress, deployerAddress, 50)`. The same value `50 bps default` is in Decision D11 and again in tech-spec Acceptance Criteria/Post-deploy Task 20 (`splitter.feeBps() returns 50`). This is light duplication of a configuration constant across three places.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) accept duplication since the deploy script genuinely needs the literal value and AC needs to assert it; or (b) introduce a `DEFAULT_FEE_BPS = 50` constant referenced from Decision D11 and the deploy script. Not blocking; flagging for awareness.

findings.description: These six pure-logic middleware tasks omit `Verify-smoke`. Per the template these fields are optional when a task is internal logic covered by tests, so omission is acceptable. However, Tasks 7 (codec) and 9 (signature verify) could meaningfully smoke-check via `vitest run <file>` to catch wiring errors before the unit-test pass in Wave 5.
findings.recommendation: Optional: add `Verify-smoke: npx vitest run packages/middleware/src/x402.ts` (or equivalent per file) for tasks 7 and 9. Not required by the rules; suggestion only.

findings.location: Task 3 (Verify Arc Testnet USDC supports EIP-3009)
findings.description: Description ends with `If the function is missing - surface to user and decide on chain switch`. This is a branching policy/risk-handling instruction inside a task description, which crosses the boundary between scope and process. The same scenario is already captured in Risks (`Arc Testnet USDC doesn't expose transferWithAuthorization as expected`).
findings.recommendation: Drop the trailing sentence; the Risks table already records the fallback (`switch chain to Base Sepolia`). The task itself should just produce the verification output.

findings.description: Section mentions `the agent will spin up a local Hardhat node forking Arc Testnet and run the integration test from Wave 5 task` and `Final Wave QA re-runs every test suite and walks through all 19+ AC items`. This is concrete and useful, but contains a fork-of-testnet plan that may not be possible (forking a testnet RPC requires the upstream RPC to support `eth_call` at historical blocks, which Arc Testnet's documentation does not confirm).
findings.recommendation: Verify Arc Testnet RPC supports the archive methods Hardhat's `--fork` mode needs. If not, document fallback (run integration test directly against live Arc Testnet, gated by `ARC_TESTNET_E2E=1` as Task 14 already specifies) â€” Task 14 already does this, so the wording in AVP can be simplified to match.

summary: Tech-spec is template-compliant and approvable. Frontmatter complete; all required sections present (Solution, Architecture with Shared resources, Decisions, Data Models, Dependencies, Testing Strategy, Agent Verification Plan, Risks, User-Spec Deviations, Acceptance Criteria, Implementation Tasks). Every Decision either cites a user-spec requirement (D1-D4, D6, D7, D11) or is marked [TECHNICAL] (D5, D8, D9, D10, D12, D13). User-Spec Deviations section is substantive (six concrete deviations + [PENDING USER APPROVAL] tag). All 20 Implementation Tasks have Description, Skill, Reviewers, Files to modify, Files to read; skill+reviewer pairs match the catalog defaults (code-writing -> code-reviewer/security-auditor/test-reviewer; infrastructure-setup -> +infrastructure-reviewer; audit/QA/deploy/post-deploy skills -> reviewers none). Tasks contain no embedded AC, TDD anchors, line-number references, or time estimates. Wave conflict scan found no two tasks in the same wave modifying the same file. Audit Wave has 3 tasks (code/security/test) with reviewers none; Final Wave has QA + Deploy + Post-deploy, all appropriate for a feature that deploys to Arc Testnet. Eight minor findings: 20-task count (flagged for user split decision), Architecture subsection heading wording, two tasks (4 and 11) that drift toward implementation-detail level, a light duplication of the 50 bps default fee value across Decision/deploy/AC, optional Verify-smoke additions for codec/verify tasks, a stray process instruction in Task 3, and an AVP statement about forking Arc Testnet that may need a fallback note. None of these block approval.
